rewrite_actions: Replaced biased/stereotypical competitor weaknesses (Accenture fluktuation, TCS cultural barriers, Arvato capacity limits) with verifiable structural disadvantages
Updated IBM positioning from outdated Consulting spin-off narrative to 2026 Co-Creation/Ecosystem focus
Updated TCS positioning from price-only to IDC MarketScape Leadership + MasterCraft automation
Integrated regulatory deadlines (NIS2 April 2026, SAP ECC EOL 2027) into differentiation statements for pipeline acceleration
Replaced unverified pricing claims (30-50% teurer) with structural TCO arguments
Added CFO-focused trap question (5-year TCO with exit costs) replacing overly narrow security clearance question
Simplified trap question 2 from compound question to focused reference-customer question

remaining_improvements: Named reference customers still missing in differentiation statements (data access limitation, not analysis quality)
T-Systems own positioning risks not transparently disclosed (acceptable for Battle Card, would need addition for Advisory Report usage)
Energiewende as business-case accelerator could be more explicitly woven into differentiations

convergence_path: Initial score CSO 3.67 / Analyst 3.5 → Iteration 2: CSO 4.17 / Analyst 4.0 (converged)
key_improvements: Bias elimination: Removed stereotypical and speculative competitor weaknesses, replaced with verifiable structural gaps
Positioning currency: Updated all competitor positionings to reflect 2026 market reality
Urgency integration: Regulatory deadlines (NIS2, ECC EOL) now embedded in differentiation statements
Stakeholder coverage: Trap questions now cover CISO, CIO, OT-Leiter, and CFO perspectives
Pricing rigor: Unverified price comparisons replaced with structural TCO arguments
